figures.caption: TABLE III COMPARISON OF QUANTITATIVE RESULTS ON KITTI EIGEN-SPLIT. (↑ INDICATES HIGHER IS BETTER, ↓ INDICATES LOWER IS BETTER.)
Fig. 6. Qualitative comparisons of results on ADE20K dataset.

Fig. 3. Overview of the EvoNAS pipeline. The framework integrates distributed multi-model parallel evaluation with multi-objective evolutionary search, enabling efficient validation of candidate architectures across GPUs and progressive approximation of the Pareto frontier in terms of accuracy, latency, and computational complexity.

Fig. 4. Performance–efficiency trade-offs of EvoNAS across four benchmarks: (a) COCO, (b) ADE20K, (c) KITTI, and (d) NYU v2. The x-axis denotes MACs, the y-axis represents task-specific accuracy metrics (mAP, mIoU, or AbsRel), bubble size indicates parameter count, and color encodes inference latency.
Fig. 5. Qualitative comparisons of results on COCO dataset.
TABLE I OBJECT DETECTION ON COCO WITH MASK R-CNN [40]. LATENCY AND THROUGHPUT ARE MEASURED AT A TEST RESOLUTION OF 800× 1280. (↑ INDICATES HIGHER IS BETTER, ↓ INDICATES LOWER IS BETTER.)
TABLE II SEMANTIC SEGMENTATION ON ADE20K WITH UPERNET [41]. LATENCY AND THROUGHPUT ARE MEASURED AT A TEST RESOLUTION OF 512× 2048. (↑ INDICATES HIGHER IS BETTER, ↓ INDICATES LOWER IS BETTER.)

Fig. 9. Evolutionary trajectories of EvoNAS on COCO, ADE20K, KITTI, and NYU v2. Top: 3D distribution of candidate architectures across generations in the accuracy–latency–MACs space. Bottom: normalized hypervolume curves showing the convergence behavior of the Pareto front over 200 generations.

Fig. 2. Overview of the two-stage supernet optimization with dual-domain knowledge distillation. Stage 1 pretrains the VSS-ViT supernet encoder on ImageNet-1K to establish a stable initialization. Stage 2 adapts the supernet to the target task by aligning student VSS/ViT features with teacher ViT features through spatial- and frequency-domain distillation, while jointly applying prediction-level and ground-truth supervision.
TABLE V EFFICIENCY VALIDATION OF THE PROPOSED PARALLEL EVALUATION FRAMEWORK. PWS DENOTES Persistent Workers. Config IS REPORTED AS (Proc./GPU, Models/Proc.).

Fig. 1. The EvoNAS unified visual architecture, featuring a searchable hybrid VSS-ViT encoder, a Spatial Mamba decoder, and optional task heads for detection, segmentation, and MDE. Searchable components within the Evo-Mamba and Evo-Transformer blocks define the encoder’s supernet search space, enabling flexible and general-purpose architecture optimization across diverse vision tasks.
TABLE IV COMPARISON OF QUANTITATIVE RESULTS ON NYU DEPTH V2 DATASET. (↑ INDICATES HIGHER IS BETTER, ↓ INDICATES LOWER IS BETTER.)
